title: PathAwareCritique
description: Set 066 — the saved multi-provider path-aware critique artifact for one session set. Written as path-aware-critique.json beside spec.md at the session-set root. Records the operator-run, multi-provider path-aware review (today GitHub Copilot driving GPT-5.4 + Gemini-Pro over the repo; the first-party tool-loop adapter is deferred to Set 067). The Set-066 S2 close-out gate confirms a valid, content-non-trivial artifact of this shape exists before a set-terminal close when the recorded pathAwareCritique policy is 'required'. This JSON Schema is the STRUCTURAL contract; the runtime gate uses the pure-Python validator ai_router.path_aware_critique.validate_path_aware_critique_artifact, which additionally enforces the multi-provider semantic rule (at least two DISTINCT providers — a constraint JSON Schema cannot express). A structurally-valid artifact with two entries from the same provider passes this schema but is rejected by the Python validator. See docs/path-aware-critique-schema.md.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. 1 = the v1 contract locked in Set 066 S1.

sessionSetName.description: The slug of the session set this critique covers (the session-set directory basename).

pathAwareCritique.description: The policy level this artifact was produced under. An artifact is only produced for 'advisory' or 'required' sets; 'none' is accepted so the enum stays a single source of truth with the per-set attribute, but a 'none' set produces no artifact in practice.

critiquedAt.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp recording when the critique was run.

blastRadius.description: Optional echo of the ai_router.blast_radius recommendation that motivated the policy (pSet / recommended / categories). Advisory metadata; no runtime gate reads it.

critiques.description: The per-provider critique entries. At least two (multi-provider). The runtime Python validator additionally requires at least two DISTINCT providers.

Critique.description: One provider's path-aware critique. provider/model/verdict are required and non-empty; each entry must be content-non-trivial (a non-empty summary OR at least one finding). Extra provider-emitted keys are tolerated (additionalProperties open).

provider.description: The provider that produced this critique (e.g. 'openai', 'google', 'anthropic'). Distinct providers across entries is what makes the artifact multi-provider.

summary.description: The critic's prose verdict / reasoning. Required (non-empty) unless the entry carries at least one finding instead.

findings.description: The critic's structured findings for this artifact. Each finding requires a non-empty description.

Finding.description: One critique finding. Only description is reliably required; severity and category are loose optional strings; extra critic-emitted keys are tolerated.
